question: According to the Camunda 8 Expressions concept page, which external specification is referenced as further reading for FEEL?

options.text: The JSR-341 Jakarta Expression Language (EL) specification.

a.text: The Next steps section of the Expressions page explicitly links to the OMG DMN specification (omg.org/spec/DMN) as further reading on FEEL.

b.text: XPath is a separate language and is not listed in the FEEL further-reading section on the Expressions page.

c.text: Drools is a different rule engine and is not referenced from the Expressions page.

d.text: Jakarta EL (JSR-341) is the Jakarta EE expression language; it is unrelated to FEEL and is not linked from the Expressions page.

explanation: The Expressions page's Next steps section links to several FEEL-related pages inside the Camunda docs (Data types, Expressions and operators, Built-in functions, the FEEL overview) plus one external link: the OMG DMN specification, which defines FEEL.
